name: Charlie Fowlkes
abstract: Charles Baker Fowlkes (February 16, 1916 – February 9, 1980) was an American baritone saxophonist who was a member of the Count Basie Orchestra for over 25 years.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Count Basie and His Orchestra
Newport Jazz Festival: Count Basie and His Orchestra
Newport Jazz Festival: Count Basie and His Orchestra

about_quotes.speaker: Duffy Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: Charlie Fowlkes, who played baritone saxophone in the band... My first night on the band... Charlie Fowlkes went to sleep on the band bus
about_quotes.doc_title: Duffy Jackson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 17, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: The saxophones were Marshall Royal, Frank Wess, Ernie Wilkins, Charlie Fowlkes on baritone, and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Butch Miles
about_quotes.evidence: We had Jimmy Forrest, we had Al Grey, we had Curtis Fuller at one point, we had Bobby Plater, Charlie Fowlkes, a great baritone saxophonist, you know, various people that passed through the band from time to time over the years.
about_quotes.doc_title: Butch Miles interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 4, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: the saxophone section at that time was excellent with Marshall Royal playing lead, Frank Wess playing either alto or the other tenor, and Charlie Fowlkes playing baritone.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 7, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Billy Mitchell
about_quotes.evidence: The saxophone section was Charlie Fowlkes on baritone, Frank Wess playing alto and tenor, Frank Foster and myself playing tenor, and Marshall of course.
about_quotes.doc_title: Billy Mitchell interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, November 16, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Snooky Young and Gerald Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: And the reeds was Marshall Royal, baritone was Charlie Fowlkes, Frank Wess, Frank Foster, and Billy Mitchell... I was in both of them
about_quotes.doc_title: Snooky Young and Gerald Wilson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995
